characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Action Girl: Any female X5. As the main title narration says of Max, "She was designed to be the perfect soldier, a human weapon." Animalistic Abilities: Max and her fellow X5s are infused with the DNA of cats, granting them superior strength, speed and senses to those of a normal human. Unfortunately, this also causes female X5s to go into heat like a cat two or three times a year. Artificial Human: The X series are artificially created people genetically engineered in various ways to be super soldiers for the US government. Cat Girl: Not in appearance, but X5s have some feline DNA, which gives them extraordinary strength and speed... andheat cycles. Max specifically steals a statuette of the Egyptian cat goddess Bast from Logan's apartment in the pilot that catches her eye because of the cat association. They also must have at least some feline scent on them, since Joshua can smell that Max is part cat when they meet. Child Soldiers: Manticore started training themveryyoung.

characters.name: Max
characters.description: A genetically engineeredSuper-Soldierdeveloped by Manticore. She was part of a breakout of her fellow experiments led by her brother Zack and escaped to Seattle. Decades later, Max works as a bike messenger by day and looks for information about her fellow transgenics by night. The Baby of the Bunch: Max was the youngest of her unit back at Manticore, and the others call her "Maxie". Badass Biker: Of both the motorbike and bicycle variety. Buxom Beauty Standard: Max isn't lacking in curves and the men around her are always appreciative of it. One episode had her convincing a meanBouncerto let her pass theWannabe Linein a club just by taking off her jacket and revealing how busty she is and another episode had her defeating atelekinetic cultistby flashing her breasts at him, making him soDistracted by the Sexyhe lost his concentration and gave her an opening to beat him. The Chosen One:As revealed in the final book, Dr Sandeman modified her DNA for this purpose, to save all theMuggles. A less fantastical version of the trope than most: her DNA is the key to immunizing people against the comet-borne virus that the Familiars hope will cause the apocalypse.

characters.name: Alec
characters.description: Another X5 transgenic and twin brother of Ben, who breaks out with Max during her second visit to Manticore. Once outside he fully embraces his freedom and becomes a bit of a hedonist, though he will still reluctantly assist Max. Atrocious Alias: Max finds his boxing stage name "Monty Cora" to be this. Backup Twin: Twinned with Ben, he becomes a series regular in Season 2 after Ben wasKilled Off for Realin Season 1. Brilliant, but Lazy: Like all X5s, Alec has a theoretically genius-level I.Q.; however, he's not big on either hard work or reading. Chivalrous Pervert: In "Borrowed Time", after sharing their issues about Max and Logan with each other at a bar, Alec and Asha go to Alec's apartment with the intention of sleeping together, but Asha passes out drunk on the couch before anything can happen. The scene appears to toy with the idea of making the audience think he might take advantage of her for a second, but instead he just puts a blanket over her.

characters.name: Zack
characters.description: Planner of the original breakout. Zack was designed to be his X5 unit's field leader and as such is naturally protective of them, to a obsessive degree. And I Must Scream: AfterManticore recaptures him, he's used as a organ donor, providing a heart to Max and liver and kidneys to others. While still alive. Big Brother Instinct: Most of hisBig Damn Heroesmoments are due to this. He also shootshimself in the head so his heart can be used to save Max. It's implied that his feelings for Max aremore than brotherly. Brainwashed and Crazy: In his last episode. Knight Templar Big Brother: Unapologetically kills Vogelsang (the information broker Max hired, who was later squeezed down by Lydecker) to protect his siblings.

characters.name: Ben
characters.description: Another escaped X5 from Max's unit and twin brother of Alec, although he dies before getting the chance to learn Alec exists. As a kid Ben use to tell stories to keep everyone's spirits up, but sadly he lost his mind after he escaped Manticore and became aSerial Killer. Ambiguous Situation: Whether or not Zack, who was keeping track of the other X5s, knew where Ben was and what he was doing. Desperately Looking for a Purpose in Life: He doesn't know how to function outside of the framework that Manticore created and tries to act out being a soldier through his hunts. Face of an Angel, Mind of a Demon: Being played byJensen Ackles, he's just as much of a blond-haired, soft-featuredPretty Boyas Alec, and doesn't look or act obviously threatening at first glance, but under the surface he's a deeply disturbed homicidal maniac. Hunting the Most Dangerous Game: He captures people and hunts them down, ripping out their teeth to prove his faith to 'The Blue Lady' (a figure the kids in Max's unit based on an image of the Virgin Mary a sympathetic janitor gave them).

characters.name: Tinga
characters.description: Another escaped X5 from Max's unit, who managed to start a new life under the alias Penny, get married to a man named Charlie Smith, and have a kid named Case. Then Manticore tracked her down. Baby Factory: Renfro's plans for her, due to her being able to pass along herSuper-Soldiertraits like enhanced IQ and spatial awareness to her children, which hadn't been the case with previous attempts at crossbreeding at Manticore. Happily Married: Until Manticore catches up with them. Hell-Bent for Leather: Like Max, Zack and Alec, she wears a leather coat. Killed Off for Real:The tank she is placed in kills her.

characters.name: Brin
characters.description: Another escaped X5 from Max's unit, who got recaptured and brainwashed into a Manticore enforcer. Consummate Professional: Once reindoctrinated, she acts like the perfect soldier Manticore always wanted. I Don't Want to Die: Said word for word, and the reason she allows Manticore to recapture her. The Dragon: Becomes one for Renfro for a while. Face–Heel Turn: After being taken back to Manticore, she is reindoctrinated and becomes "one of them now", as Tinga put it.

characters.name: Joshua
characters.description: Joshua is the first transgenic developed, a human/dog hybrid, who breaks out with Max during her second trip to Manticore. Due to his canine features Joshua can't be seen outside, which causes a lot of friction between him and Max as he wants to explore but she wants to keep him safe. Beast and Beauty: The Beast to Max's and Annie's Beauty. Beast Man: Part dog, part man. Beware the Nice Ones: He's pretty damn scary when he gets his hands on Ames White, who murdered Annie. Max tells him he has to let him live for now in "Freak Nation" because of how it would look, but he finally gets his revenge in the third novel,After the Dark, twisting White's head clean off. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: Sometimes gives off this vibe because of his naivety.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A human/lizard transgenic designed for desert combat. After escaping Manticore, Mole becomes the unofficial leader of the underground transgenic community in Terminal City, coordinating efforts to help other transgenics. Cigar Chomper: Like aFilm Noirprotagonist if they were a lizardman. Deadpan Snarker: He's very gruff and sarcastic. In the novelAfter the Dark(which is set during Yuletime), when Max tells him not to kill Lydecker, he says, "But it's what I want for Christmas." Non-Indicative Name: Named Mole, even though he looks like a lizard instead of a mole. Rebel Leader: A role Mole grows into gradually as more transgenics are found.

characters.name: Krit and Syl
characters.description: Cain and Abel: Krit's twin Devon is one of the X5s still with and loyally serving Manticore after Krit's escape. The Cavalry: They help with the raid on Manticore in the first season finale, after only being mentioned or seen in flashbacks before then. Really Gets Around: Syl is said to be constantly having her heart broken or breaking others hearts. Unseen No More: They are often mentioned throughout season 1 but their only adult scenes are in the season finale. What the Hell, Hero?: Neither are happy with Max when she first proposes allying with Lydecker, although they hear her out and ultimately do it.

characters.name: Jace
characters.description: Letting Her Hair Down: She lets her hair flow freely for the first time after finally deserting. Perpetual Frowner: She tends to look unhappy and hostile regardless of context until the end of her episode. Pregnant Badass: She is going to have a baby and is a skilled fighter and adapter during her rencounter with Max, whom she almost beats. Deconstructed a bit when the strain makes her faint in a fight.

characters.name: Logan
characters.description: A journalist from a wealthy family who uses his resources to be "Eyes Only", a faceless hacker who interrupts tv signals to report on corrupt dealings in Seattle with impunity. Max first runs into Logan when she tries to steal from his apartment. In exchange for his tracking down lost transgenics, Max does missions for Logan to help out Seattle's less fortunate. The Alleged Car: A Pontiac Aztek. Kind of pathetic given Logan's money, but it's best to keeping a low profile on missions. Catchphrase: As Eyes Only: "This is a streaming freedom video bulletin. It cannot be traced, it cannot be stopped and it is the only free voice left in this city... This has been a streaming freedom video bulletin. Peace, out." Genius Cripple: His spine is damaged in the very first episode, leaving him paraplegic. He is still invaluable as a hacker and general computer whizz. Green-Eyed Monster: Only on one occasion, but he jumps to the conclusion thatMax and Alec are in a relationship based on seeing them having a friendly conversation and a hug outside Max's apartment. He then gets drunk and confronts Max with his suspicions.

characters.name: Cindy
characters.description: Max's first and best friend, co-worker and roommate. Original Cindy always has Max's back, and after she learns Max's secret, she regularly runs interference for her. A Day in the Limelight: "Shortie's in Love" and "Rising" are the only episodes in either season to give her character any kind of real focus. Demoted to Extra: Got hit with this the hardest in Season 2 compared to her importance in Season 1. But remained the most prominent of Max's human co-workers, since she was the only one who knew the truth about her and whom Max could talk with openly about transgenic issues and the virus keeping her and Logan apart. She also knew about and got to be friends with Alec and Joshua. Dope Slap: Called upon occasionally to give these to Max; see "Meow" for a particularly good example when Max's heat cycle is making her put a move onNormalof all people (Max: "Thanks. I needed that."). Meaningful Rename: From Cynthia to Original Cindy, shedding her timid childhood persona.

characters.name: Normal
characters.description: The unpleasant boss of Jam Pony Express, the bike messenger service Max, Original Cindy and Alec work for. In Season 2 he is one of the most fervent anti-transgenic people. Ambiguously Bi: In "Fuhgeddaboudit" when Alec temporarily takes up boxing again, Normal mentions having very homoerotic-sounding dreams about Alec as a gladiator and Normal as his valet, wiping his sword, rubbing his muscles... Bad Boss: Overworks and belittles his employees. They in turn give him no respect. Deadpan Snarker: Especially to his workers. Delivery Guy: He helps Gem deliver her daughter Eve in the Season 2 finale.

characters.name: Kendra
characters.description: Max's original roommate and a friend of hers and Cindy's. Was mostly concerned with Max's love life. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: She disappears late in Season 1 when she moves in with her boyfriend Walter and is never heard from again. Original Cindy, who originally had her own place, moves into Max's place in her place. Ditzy Genius: In the prequel novel, Max muses she is a competent translator who can stick up for herself but also is kind of an airhead (being a horrible judge of distance and thinking it is fine to live in a condemned building).

characters.name: Asha
characters.description: A member of a resistance cell that often collaborates with Logan. Dark and Troubled Past:All There in the Manualsources say her mother was a former reform mayoral candidate until being murdered by the machine politicians along with Asha's brother, while her father was anIntrepid Reporterwhose "untimely" death may have been another murder. Locked Out of the Loop: Like many of Logan's contacts, she believes he is a collaborator of Eyes Only rather than the same man. Pair the Spares: Looked like she and Alec were going this way, until they didn't. La Résistance: An important member of theS1W, who fight against police brutality and corruption in Seattle.

characters.name: Sketchy
characters.description: Another employee at Jam Pony. Prone to slacking off, cutting corners, and getting into trouble. Distressed Dude: Often had to be bailed out by Max after doing something stupid. Early-Installment Weirdness: Sketchy was much sleazier in the pilot than the rest of the show. He also had a fiancée named Natalie, but in later episodes he was single without explanation. Shout-Out: Named after the threeChipmunks. The Slacker: Sketchy likes to cut corners and avoid doing work.

characters.name: Herbal
characters.description: Another Jam Pony messenger. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappeared without a trace between seasons 1 and 2. Closer to Earth: Probably the wisest Jam Pony employee, even if he was as much of a goof-off as the rest of them. Erudite Stoner: Not shown, but at least implied in his name that he smokes.

characters.name: Colonel Lydecker
characters.description: Manticore goon in charge of tracking down the X5 series. Lydecker was originally in charge of the X5s' training and as such sees them as his children. Abusive Parent: Closest thing the X5s had to a parent, and he put them through grueling exercises to mold them into soldiers since the time they were small children. The Alcoholic: Hasn't had a drink for 10 years when the show starts, but that doesn't last the season finale. Big Bad: Of Season One, until Renfro turns out to beEviler than Thouin the last couple episodes. Colonel Badass: He is a powerful military colonel with lots of authority Although following theConservation of Ninjutsu, his badassery goes up exponentially when he's by himself.

characters.name: Agent Ames
characters.description: Head enforcer of the conspiracy that wants to hunt down Max and the rest of the transgenics. His job as an FBI agent allows him to do so under legal auspices. Antagonistic Offspring: It turns out late in Season 2 that Dr Sandeman was his father. Sandeman turned against the breeding cult's agenda after his other son C.J. proved to be a regular human who would be vulnerable to the virus, while Ames remained a fervent believer (and changed his surname to White). Big Bad: In Season Two from the second episode on, after Renfro dies in the premiere. Max lampshades it: "You must be the new bad guy in my life." Cain and Abel: The Cain to C.J.'s Abel. Also with Joshua; although they hadn't met until recently, they both had Sandeman as a father. Contrasting Sequel Antagonist: While Lydecker and Renfro worked for a black ops military program and wanted to recapture Max alive if possible, White works for anAncient Conspiracyand just plain wants to kill her and the rest of the transgenics.

characters.name: Director Renfro
characters.description: Head of Manticore, really wants the X5s back, enjoys threatening Lydecker. Disc-One Final Boss: He is the big threat of Season 2. Until Manticore is deactivated well before the finale. Eviler than Thou: She ousts Lydecker from his position of season oneBig Bad. Excessive Evil Eyeshadow: A live-action version! Her eye shadow has more color than her eyes, and she does some very bad things until her last scene. Expy: OfNana Visitor's character the Intendant inStar Trek: Deep Space Nine, theEvil Counterpartof her character Colonel Kira Nerys from theMirror Universe. Minus the Intendant's sexualized aspects.
